name: validation-summary
role: Error-summary panel rendered at the top of an invalid form. Lists each field-level error as an anchor link pointing to its field. WCAG 2.4.6 (Headings and Labels), 3.3.1 (Error Identification), 3.3.3 (Error Suggestion). Becomes the focus target after a failed submit so screen readers immediately announce the count and contents. Renders as a <section role='alert' aria-labelledby='<id>'> with a heading + count + ordered list of links.

semanticStructure: There are 3 errors in this form
• Password must be at least 8 characters
• You must agree to the terms

tones.error: Default tone. role='alert' for assertive announcement. Used when submit failed validation.
tones.warning: role='status' instead of 'alert'. Used when form has soft warnings (e.g., 'password is short but allowed').
tones.info: role='status'. Used for help text at top of form ('Fields marked * are required').
tones.success: role='status'. Used after successful submit ('Saved — your changes are live').

focusManagement.afterFailedSubmit: On submit-failed: programmatically focus the validation-summary via .focus() (which works because tabindex='-1' makes it programmatically focusable). Screen readers will then announce the entire summary as the user's reading context — heading + count + first link.
focusManagement.skipToError: Each <li><a> in the summary anchors to its field via href='#<field-id>'. Clicking jumps focus to the field. Use scroll-margin-top on the field to offset for sticky headers.
focusManagement.dontStealFocus: Only steal focus on submit-failed, NOT on every keystroke validation. Live validation should announce via the field's aria-describedby, not by moving focus.

ariaPatterns.roleAlert: <section role='alert'> for error tone — assertive announcement so users immediately know submit failed.
ariaPatterns.ariaLabelledby: aria-labelledby points to the heading inside, giving the section a programmatic name.
ariaPatterns.tabindexMinusOne: tabindex='-1' makes the section focusable via JS but not in the natural tab order.
ariaPatterns.ariaLive: role='alert' implies aria-live='assertive'. Don't set aria-live='polite' here — error feedback should interrupt.

name: validation-message
role: Single inline message below a form-control. Three variants — error (validation failed), help (instructional hint always shown), success (positive confirmation, e.g., 'Username available'). Connected to the field via aria-describedby. Optional leading icon + descriptive text. Used for both pre-submit live validation AND post-submit error display.

variants.error: Used when the field's aria-invalid='true'. role='alert' on the message when shown post-submit; no role on live-validation messages (the field's aria-describedby handles announcement).
variants.help: Always shown. Instructional hint ('Use at least 8 characters'). Not announced as an alert — just connected via aria-describedby.
variants.success: Positive feedback ('Username available' / 'Password strong'). Shown live during input.
variants.warning: Soft warning that doesn't block submit ('Password is short but allowed').

ariaPatterns.ariaDescribedby: The field's aria-describedby must reference the message's id. Multiple aria-describedby targets are space-separated: aria-describedby='email-help email-error'.
ariaPatterns.messageOnSubmit: Post-submit error messages get role='alert' temporarily so AT announces them. Once user starts editing the field, the message reverts to plain text (aria-describedby still connects it).
ariaPatterns.iconAriaHidden: Icons inside validation-messages are decorative (aria-hidden='true'). The text content carries the meaning.

errorMessageGuidelines: Be specific. 'Email is required' beats 'Field is required'.
Be constructive. 'Use at least 8 characters' beats 'Password too short'.
Don't repeat the field label. The label says 'Email'; the error says 'Email is required' (not 'Email — Email is required').
Phrase as instruction or fact, not blame. 'Choose a different username — that one is taken' beats 'Username invalid'.
Match the input language. If field accepts dates as 'MM/DD/YYYY', error should mirror that format.

name: validation-status-icon
role: Small inline status icon used inside form-control trailing slot or beside field labels in the validation-summary. Indicates validation status — error (✕) / valid (✓) / warning (⚠) / validating (spinner). Always aria-hidden because the field's aria-invalid + aria-describedby handle AT communication.

statuses.valid: ✓ in --success. Shown when field passed live validation (e.g., 'Username available').
statuses.validating: Spinning ⟳ in --text-recede. Shown during async validation (e.g., 'Checking username…').

ariaPatterns.alwaysAriaHidden: Always aria-hidden='true' + focusable='false'. The icon reinforces visually; the field's ARIA state communicates to AT.
ariaPatterns.validatingSpinner: When status='validating', the spin animation should collapse to none under prefers-reduced-motion: reduce.

name: validation-orchestrator
role: Invisible wrapper primitive that coordinates form-level validation state across child form-controls. Renders as <form data-state='<pattern-state>' novalidate>. Sets data-state on itself based on field-level state aggregation. JS-side: tracks pristine/touched/validating/valid/invalid; on submit, runs validation, prevents default if invalid, focuses the validation-summary if added. The DOM contract is documented here; the JS implementation is host-provided in v1 (a reference JS implementation arrives in a P1 utility pack).

states.default: Pristine — form has never been interacted with. No validation messages shown. Submit button enabled (clicking it will trigger validation). 'default' is the canonical state name per the catalog convention; 'pristine' is the descriptive term used in the docs.
states.touched: User has focused-and-blurred at least one field, but not submitted. Field-level live validation may be running per validate-on policy.
states.validating: Async validation in flight on at least one field. Affected fields show validating status icon. Submit is aria-busy.
states.valid: All fields pass validation. Submit button is fully enabled.
states.invalid: One or more fields fail validation. Field-level messages visible on touched-and-failed fields. Submit attempt would scroll-and-focus the summary.
states.submitted: Form successfully submitted. Often replaced with a success view; validation-summary may render in tone='success'.
states.submit-failed: Server rejected the submission (validation failed server-side OR network/auth error). validation-summary renders with role='alert' and is focused programmatically.

validateOnPolicies.submit: Only validate on submit. No live feedback. Used for short forms.
validateOnPolicies.blur: Validate each field as it blurs. Used for forms where users want immediate feedback per field.
validateOnPolicies.input: Validate continuously as user types. Aggressive; mostly used for password strength meters.
validateOnPolicies.submit-then-blur: Don't validate until first submit attempt. After that, validate each field on blur. The 80% recommendation (per Nielsen Norman + WAI-ARIA APG).

ariaPatterns.novalidate: <form novalidate> — disables browser-native validation so this pattern's JS owns all messaging. Required for consistent cross-browser behaviour.
ariaPatterns.preventDefaultOnInvalid: On submit, JS calls e.preventDefault() if any field's aria-invalid='true'. Then focus is moved to the validation-summary.
ariaPatterns.afterServerError: Server-side errors map to field-level aria-invalid + aria-describedby pointing to a fresh validation-message. Plus a top-level validation-summary listing all server errors.
